NAME

server.h - ascii-chat Client Server Connection Management Interface

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Server Connection Management Interface

Provides connection establishment, management, and thread-safe packet transmission functions for communicating with ascii-chat servers.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Definition in file src/client/server.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum connection_error_t

Connection establishment error codes. These codes are returned by server_connection_establish() to indicate different failure modes. Zero indicates success.

Enumerator

CONNECTION_SUCCESS
Connection established successfully.
CONNECTION_WARNING_NO_CLIENT_AUTH
Server not using client verification (warning).
CONNECTION_ERROR_GENERIC
Generic error (retry allowed).
CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED
Authentication failure (no retry).
CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED
Host key verification failed (no retry).

36   CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED = -3

void
  server_connection_cleanup ()


Cleanup connection management subsystem. Closes any active
    connection and destroys synchronization objects. Called during client
    shutdown.

void
  server_connection_close ()


Close server connection gracefully. Close server connection
    gracefully.




Marks connection as inactive and closes the socket. This function
    is safe to call multiple times and from multiple threads.


Definition at line 809 of file
  src/client/server.c.


809                                {
810   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() called");
811 
812   // Acquire send mutex to prevent race with threaded_send_image_frame()
813   // This ensures any in-flight sends complete before we destroy the transport
814   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
815   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
816 
817   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
818   if (g_client_transport) {
819     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Destroying transport=%p (is_connected=%s) before closing socket",
820               (void *)g_client_transport, acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) ? "true" : "false");
821     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
822     g_client_transport = NULL;
823     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Transport destroyed, g_client_transport set to NULL");
824   }
825 
826   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
827

829     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Closing socket: %d", (int)g_sockfd);
830     close_socket(g_sockfd);
831     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
832     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Socket closed");
833   }
834 
835   g_my_client_id = 0;
836

839     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Cleaning up crypto context");
840     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
841     g_encryption_enabled = false;
842   }
843 
844   // Turn ON terminal logging when connection is closed (unless it was disabled with --quiet)
845   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
846     log_set_terminal_output(true);
847   }
848   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() completed");

int
  server_connection_establish (const char * address, int port, int
  reconnect_attempt, bool first_connection, bool
  has_ever_connected)


Establish connection to ascii-chat server. Establishes a TCP
    connection to the server and performs the cryptographic handshake. This
    function handles DNS resolution, socket creation, connection establishment,
    and authentication. It is designed for use in a reconnection loop with
    exponential backoff.




Parameters


address Server IP address, hostname, or ACDS
  session string (e.g., "localhost", "192.168.1.100", or
  "blue-mountain-tiger")


port Server port number (typically 27224)


reconnect_attempt Current reconnection attempt number (0 for first
  attempt). Used to track retry count for logging and backoff calculation.


first_connection Whether this is the client's first attempt to connect.
  If true, displays more informative messages. If false, this is a reconnection
  attempt after previous connection loss.


has_ever_connected Whether the client has EVER successfully connected in
  this session. Used to distinguish "first attempt ever" from
  "reconnection attempt".


Returns


Connection establishment status:

    
  
  • CONNECTION_SUCCESS (0): Connection established and authenticated
      successfully
    • 
  
  • CONNECTION_WARNING_NO_CLIENT_AUTH (1): Connected but server is not
      verifying client identity
    • 
  
  • CONNECTION_ERROR_GENERIC (-1): Network error (resolve failed, connect
      refused, timeout). Caller should retry with exponential backoff.
    • 
  
  • CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED (-2): Authentication failed (wrong password,
      bad SSH key). Caller should NOT retry - user intervention required.
    • 
  
  • CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED (-3): Server host key verification
      failed. Caller should NOT retry - user must update known_hosts.
    • 









Connection
  Lifecycle



  
1.

  
Resolution: Resolves hostname/IP to socket address (supports IPv4
      and IPv6)

  
2.

  
Socket Creation: Creates TCP socket with appropriate options

  
3.

  
Connection: Attempts TCP connect with timeout

  
4.

  
Handshake: Performs cryptographic handshake (X25519 key exchange,
      Ed25519 auth)

  
5.

  
Initialization: Sends client capabilities and client join
    packet

  
6.

  
State Update: Updates global connection state atomically








Error
  Recovery


Retryable (CONNECTION_ERROR_GENERIC):


    
  
  • Connection refused: Server not listening yet
    • 
  
  • Connection timeout: Network slow or firewall blocking
    • 
  
  • DNS resolution failure: Temporary DNS issue
    • 
  
  • Socket creation failure: System resource exhaustion
    • 



Caller should implement exponential backoff:




int result = server_connection_establish(address, port, attempt, first, ever_connected);
if (result == CONNECTION_ERROR_GENERIC) {


  // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 210ms, 410ms, 610ms, ..., capped at 5 seconds


  int delay_ms = 10 + (200 * attempt);


  if (delay_ms > 5000) delay_ms = 5000;


  sleep_ms(delay_ms);


  attempt++;


  continue;  // Retry
}



Non-Retryable (AUTH or HOST_KEY failures):


    
  
  • CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED: Password or SSH key doesn't match
      server's expectations
    • 
  
  • CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED: Server's public key not in
      client's known_hosts
    • 



These indicate configuration problems that won't be fixed by
    waiting or retrying.




See also


server_connection_is_active "Check connection
  status"



server_connection_close "Close connection
    gracefully"




server_connection_lost "Signal connection loss (called
    by other threads)"




topic_client_connection "Connection Management
    Architecture"




Attempts to connect to the specified server with full capability
    negotiation. Implements reconnection logic with exponential backoff for
    failed attempts. On successful connection, performs initial handshake
    including terminal capabilities and client join protocol.




Parameters


address Server IP address or hostname


port Server port number


reconnect_attempt Current reconnection attempt number (0 for first)


first_connection True if this is the initial connection attempt


has_ever_connected True if a connection was ever successfully
  established


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 355 of file
  src/client/server.c.


356                                                          {
357   (void)first_connection; // Currently unused
358   if (!address || port <= 0) {
359     log_error("Invalid address or port parameters");
360     return -1;
361   }
362 
363   // Close any existing connection
364   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
365     close_socket(g_sockfd);
366     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
367   }
368 
369   // Apply reconnection delay if this is a retry
370   if (reconnect_attempt > 0) {
371     unsigned int delay_us = get_reconnect_delay(reconnect_attempt);
372     // Reconnection attempt logged only to file
373     platform_sleep_us(delay_us);
374 
375     // Check if user requested exit during reconnection delay
376     if (should_exit()) {
377       log_debug("Exit requested during reconnection delay");
378       return -1;
379     }
380   } else {
381     // Initial connection logged only to file
382   }
383 
384   connection_endpoint_t endpoint = {0};
385   asciichat_error_t endpoint_result = connection_endpoint_resolve(address, port, &endpoint);
386   if (endpoint_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
387     log_error("Failed to resolve connection endpoint: %s", asciichat_error_string(endpoint_result));
388     return -1;
389   }
390 
391   // Check for WebSocket URL - handle separately from TCP
392   if (endpoint.protocol == CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET) {
393     // WebSocket connection - bypass TCP socket creation
394     // Use the original address (URL already contains port if specified)
395     const char *ws_url = endpoint.input;
396     log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%u)", ws_url, endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
397 
398     // Set server IP and port for crypto handshake
399     // This ensures the crypto context has proper server address info for known_hosts verification
400     server_connection_set_ip(endpoint.host);
401     server_connection_set_port(endpoint.port);
402     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Set server IP=%s, port=%u from WebSocket endpoint", endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
403 
404     // Initialize crypto if encryption is enabled
405     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
406     if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
407       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
408       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
409       return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
410     }
411 
412     // Get crypto context for transport
413     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
414 
415     // Create WebSocket transport through the shared factory.
416     asciichat_error_t ws_result =
417         connection_factory_open("client", ws_url, 0, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, &g_client_transport, &endpoint);
418     if (ws_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
419       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
420       return -1;
421     }
422     if (!g_client_transport) {
423       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
424       return -1;
425     }
426     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created WebSocket ACIP transport with crypto context");
427 
428     // Set connection as active
429     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
430     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
431 
432     // CRITICAL: Wait for WebSocket connection to be established before sending
433     // The service thread is running but may not have completed TLS handshake yet
434     // Don't block indefinitely - use timeout loops to allow responsiveness
435     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Waiting for WebSocket connection to be established...");
436     const int CONNECT_CHECK_ATTEMPTS = 300; // 300 * 50ms = 15 seconds timeout
437     int connect_attempts = 0;
438     while (!acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) && connect_attempts < CONNECT_CHECK_ATTEMPTS) {
439       platform_sleep_us(50000); // Sleep 50ms between checks
440       connect_attempts++;
441       if (connect_attempts % 20 == 0) { // Log every 1 second (20 * 50ms)
442         log_info("CLIENT_CONNECT: Waiting for connection... (%d seconds elapsed)", connect_attempts / 20);
443       }
444     }
445 
446     if (!acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport)) {
447       log_error("Failed to establish WebSocket connection within 15 seconds");
448       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
449       g_client_transport = NULL;
450       atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
451       return -1;
452     }
453 
454     log_info("CLIENT_CONNECT: WebSocket connection established, proceeding with handshake");
455 
456     // Send initial terminal capabilities to server
457     int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
458                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
459     if (result < 0) {
460       log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
461       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
462       g_client_transport = NULL;
463       return -1;
464     }
465 
466     // Disable terminal logging after initial setup (for non-snapshot mode)
467     if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
468       log_set_terminal_output(false);
469     }
470 
471     // Build capabilities flags
472     uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO;
473     log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
474     if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
475       log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
476       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
477     }
478     if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
479       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
480     }
481     if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
482       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
483     }
484 
485     // Generate display name from username + PID
486     const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
487     char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
488     int pid = getpid();
489     SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
490     if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
491       log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
492       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
493       g_client_transport = NULL;
494       return -1;
495     }
496 
497     log_info("WebSocket connection established successfully");
498     return 0;
499   }
500 
501   asciichat_error_t connect_result =
502       connection_factory_open("client", address, (uint16_t)port, NULL, &g_client_transport, &endpoint);
503   if (connect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !g_client_transport) {
504     log_error("Failed to establish TCP transport: %s", asciichat_error_string(connect_result));
505     return -1;
506   }
507 
508   g_sockfd = g_client_transport->methods->get_socket(g_client_transport);
509   if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
510     log_error("TCP transport did not expose a valid socket");
511     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
512     g_client_transport = NULL;
513     return -1;
514   }
515 
516   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, endpoint.host, sizeof(g_server_ip));
517   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Resolved TCP endpoint host=%s port=%u", endpoint.host, endpoint.port);
518 
519   // Connection successful - extract local port for client ID
520   struct sockaddr_storage local_addr = {0};
521   socklen_t addr_len = sizeof(local_addr);
522   if (getsockname(g_sockfd, (struct sockaddr *)&local_addr, &addr_len) == -1) {
523     log_error("Failed to get local socket address: %s", network_error_string());
524     close_socket(g_sockfd);
525     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
526     return -1;
527   }
528 
529   // Extract port from either IPv4 or IPv6 address
530   int local_port = 0;
531   if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET) {
532     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in *)&local_addr)->sin_port);
533   } else if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET6) {
534     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in6 *)&local_addr)->sin6_port);
535   }
536   g_my_client_id = (uint32_t)local_port;
537 
538   // Mark connection as active immediately after successful socket connection
539   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
540   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
541   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_reconnect, false);
542 
543   // Initialize crypto BEFORE starting protocol handshake
544   // Note: server IP is already set above in the connection loop
545   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
546   if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
547     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
548     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
549     close_socket(g_sockfd);
550     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
551     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // SSH key password was wrong - no retry
552   }
553 
554   // Create the persistent TCP transport before handshake.
555   // It starts with NULL crypto; client_crypto_handshake() sets crypto_ctx on success.
556   g_client_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("client", g_sockfd, NULL);
557   if (!g_client_transport) {
558     log_error("Failed to create TCP ACIP transport");
559     close_socket(g_sockfd);
560     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
561     return -1;
562   }
563   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created TCP ACIP transport (crypto pending handshake)");
564 
565   // Perform crypto handshake if encryption is enabled
566   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_handshake()");
567   int handshake_result = client_crypto_handshake(g_client_transport);
568   if (handshake_result != 0) {
569     log_error("Crypto handshake failed");
570     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() failed with code %d", handshake_result);
571     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
572     g_client_transport = NULL;
573     close_socket(g_sockfd);
574     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
575     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE,
576           "Crypto handshake failed with server - this usually indicates a protocol mismatch or network issue");
577   }
578   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() succeeded");
579 
580   // Turn OFF terminal logging when successfully connected to server
581   // First connection - we'll disable logging after main.c shows the "Connected successfully" message
582   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
583     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging will be disabled after initial setup");
584   } else {
585     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging kept enabled for snapshot mode");
586   }
587 
588   // Configure socket options for optimal performance
589   if (socket_set_keepalive(g_sockfd, true) < 0) {
590     log_warn("Failed to set socket keepalive: %s", network_error_string());
591   }
592 
593   // Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal performance
594   asciichat_error_t sock_config_result = socket_configure_buffers(g_sockfd);
595   if (sock_config_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
596     log_warn("Failed to configure socket: %s", network_error_string());
597   }
598 
599   // Send initial terminal capabilities to server (this may generate debug logs)
600   int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
601                                                             (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
602   if (result < 0) {
603     log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
604     close_socket(g_sockfd);
605     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
606     return -1;
607   }
608 
609   // Now disable terminal logging after capabilities are sent (for reconnections)
610   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
611     log_set_terminal_output(false);
612     log_debug("Reconnected to server - terminal logging disabled to prevent interference with ASCII display");
613   }
614 
615   // Send client join packet for multi-user support
616   uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO; // Basic video capability
617   log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
618   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
619     log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
620     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
621   }
622   if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
623     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
624   }
625   if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
626     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
627   }
628 
629   // Generate display name from username + PID
630   const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
631 
632   char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
633   int pid = getpid();
634   SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
635 
636   if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
637     log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
638     close_socket(g_sockfd);
639     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
640     return -1;
641   }
642 
643   // Connection already marked as active after socket creation
644 
645   return 0;
646 }

void
  server_connection_shutdown ()


Emergency shutdown for signal handlers. Emergency shutdown for
    signal handlers.




Performs immediate connection shutdown without waiting for
    graceful close procedures. Uses socket shutdown to interrupt any blocking
    recv() operations in other threads.


Definition at line 896 of file
  src/client/server.c.


896                                   {
897   // NOTE: This function may be called from:
898   //   - Signal handlers on Unix (async-signal-safe context)
899   //   - SetConsoleCtrlHandler callback thread on Windows (separate thread context)
900   // Only use atomic operations and simple system calls - NO mutex locks, NO malloc, NO logging.
901 
902   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
903   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, true);
904 
905   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
906     // Only shutdown() the socket to interrupt blocking recv()/send() operations.
907     // Do NOT close() here - on Windows, closing the socket while another thread
908     // is using it is undefined behavior and can cause STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN.
909     // The actual socket close happens in server_connection_close() which is called
910     // from the main thread after worker threads have been joined.
911     socket_shutdown(g_sockfd, SHUT_RDWR);
912   }
913 
914   // For WebSocket transport: interrupt blocking recv() calls
915   // The recv() checks is_connected before waiting for data. Setting this to false
916   // will cause any blocked recv() to return immediately with CONNECTION_CLOSED.
917   // We use a function that's async-signal-safe (no mutexes) to do this.
918   server_connection_interrupt_websocket_recv();
919 
920   // DO NOT call log_set_terminal_output() here - it uses mutex which is NOT async-signal-safe.
921   // The normal cleanup path in shutdown_client() will handle logging state.
922 }

    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, and socket_shutdown().

1042     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);

1045

1051 
1052   // Unlock after send completes

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_audio_opus (const
  uint8_t * opus_data, size_t opus_size, int sample_rate, int
  frame_duration)


Thread-safe Opus audio frame transmission. Sends a single
    Opus-encoded audio frame to the server with proper synchronization and
    encryption support.




Parameters


opus_data Opus-encoded audio data


opus_size Size of encoded frame


sample_rate Sample rate in Hz


frame_duration Frame duration in milliseconds


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sends a single Opus-encoded audio frame to the server with proper
    synchronization and encryption support.




Parameters


opus_data Opus-encoded audio data


opus_size Size of encoded frame


sample_rate Sample rate in Hz


frame_duration Frame duration in milliseconds


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1078 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1079                                                                {
1080   // Get transport reference while holding mutex (brief lock)
1081   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1082 
1083   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1084   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1085     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1086     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1087   }
1088 
1089   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1090   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1091   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1092 
1093   // Build Opus packet with header (outside mutex - no blocking I/O yet)
1094   size_t header_size = 16; // sample_rate (4), frame_duration (4), reserved (8)
1095   size_t total_size = header_size + opus_size;
1096   void *packet_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, total_size);
1097   if (!packet_data) {
1098     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus packet: %zu bytes", total_size);
1099   }
1100 
1101   // Write header in network byte order
1102   uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)packet_data;
1103   uint32_t sr = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)sample_rate);
1104   uint32_t fd = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)frame_duration);
1105   memcpy(buf, &sr, 4);
1106   memcpy(buf + 4, &fd, 4);
1107   memset(buf + 8, 0, 8); // Reserved
1108 
1109   // Copy Opus data
1110   memcpy(buf + header_size, opus_data, opus_size);
1111 
1112   // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex to prevent deadlock on TCP buffer full
1113   asciichat_error_t result =
1114       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, packet_data, total_size, 0);
1115 
1116   // Clean up
1117   buffer_pool_free(NULL, packet_data, total_size);
1118 
1119   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1120   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1121     server_connection_lost();
1122     return result;
1123   }
1124 
1125   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1126 }

1468                                                                                       {
1469   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()

1471   // Build CLIENT_JOIN packet locally
1472   client_info_packet_t join_packet;
1473   SAFE_MEMSET(&join_packet, sizeof(join_packet), 0, sizeof(join_packet));
1474   join_packet.client_id = HOST_TO_NET_U32(0); // Will be assigned by server
1475   SAFE_SNPRINTF(join_packet.display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN, "%s", display_name ? display_name : "Unknown");
1476   join_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(capabilities);
1477 
1478   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1479   int send_result = threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_JOIN, &join_packet, sizeof(join_packet));
1480   if (send_result == 0) {
1481     mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1482     bool active = atomic_load_u64(&g_connection_active);
1483     socket_t socket_snapshot = g_sockfd;
1484     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
1485     if (active && socket_snapshot != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1486       (void)log_network_message(
1487           socket_snapshot, (const struct crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, LOG_INFO, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER,
1488           "CLIENT_JOIN sent (display=\"%s\", capabilities=0x%x)", join_packet.display_name, capabilities);
1489     }
1490     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1491   }
1492   return send_result;
1493 }

    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_lost(), and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t threaded_send_image_frame_h265 (const
  void * pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)


Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (thread-safe). Encodes
    RGB frame using H.265 codec before transmission. Much more
    bandwidth-efficient than raw pixel transmission. Uses mutex to prevent
    interleaving with other packet sends.




Parameters


pixel_data RGB pixel data from camera


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1231 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1231                                                                                                           {
1232   if (!pixel_data) {
1233     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pixel data is NULL");
1234   }
1235 
1236   // Get encoder - may be NULL if not initialized
1237   h265_encoder_t *encoder = g_h265_encoder;
1238   if (!encoder) {
1239     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "H.265 encoder not initialized");
1240   }
1241 
1242   // Allocate buffer and encode frame WITHOUT holding the send mutex.
1243   // This avoids long critical section that can cause deadlocks with named registry operations.
1244   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_1] Allocating buffer and encoding frame (no mutex held)");
1245   size_t output_buffer_size = 5 + (width * height * 2);
1246   uint8_t *output_buffer = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, output_buffer_size);
1247   if (!output_buffer) {
1248     log_error("Failed to allocate output buffer for frame");
1249     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate H.265 output buffer: %zu bytes", output_buffer_size);
1250   }
1251 
1252   // Encode frame
1253   size_t encoded_size = output_buffer_size - 5;
1254   asciichat_error_t encode_result = h265_encode(encoder, (uint16_t)width, (uint16_t)height, (const uint8_t *)pixel_data,
1255                                                 output_buffer + 5, &encoded_size);
1256   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_2] ✅ Frame encoded, encoded_size=%zu, result=%d", encoded_size, encode_result);
1257 
1258   if (encode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1259     log_error("H.265 encoding failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(encode_result));
1260     buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
1261     return encode_result;
1262   }
1263 
1264   // Prepare packet header: [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_data...]
1265   output_buffer[0] = 0; // flags: no flags set by default
1266   output_buffer[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
1267   output_buffer[2] = width & 0xFF;
1268   output_buffer[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
1269   output_buffer[4] = height & 0xFF;
1270 
1271   size_t total_size = 5 + encoded_size;
1272 
1273   // NOW acquire the send mutex just for the actual network transmission
1274   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_3] About to acquire g_send_mutex for network send");
1275   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1276   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_4] ✅ Acquired g_send_mutex, checking transport");
1277 
1278   // Get transport reference - may be NULL if connection was lost
1279   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1280   if (!transport || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1281     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1282     log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_5] Transport unavailable, releasing mutex");
1283     buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
1284     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport unavailable");
1285   }
1286 
1287   // Send H.265 frame with socket lock held (short critical section)
1288   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_6] Sending packet via transport (%zu bytes)", total_size);
1289   asciichat_error_t result =
1290       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265, output_buffer, total_size, 0);
1291   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_7] packet_send_via_transport returned: %d", result);
1292 
1293   // Unlock after send completes
1294   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1295   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_8] ✅ Released g_send_mutex");
1296 
1297   buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
1298   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_9] ✅ Freed output buffer");
1299 
1300   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1301   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1302     server_connection_lost();
1303     return result;
1304   }
1305 
1306   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1307 }

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_packet
  (packet_type_t type, const void * data, size_t len)


Thread-safe packet transmission. Sends a packet to the server with
    proper mutex protection and connection state checking. Automatically handles
    encryption if crypto is ready.




Parameters


type Packet type identifier


data Packet payload


len Payload length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sends a packet to the server with proper mutex protection and
    connection state checking. Automatically handles encryption if crypto is
    ready.




Parameters


type Packet type identifier


data Packet payload


len Payload length


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 989 of file
  src/client/server.c.


989                                                                                          {
990   // Lock mutex for entire send operation to prevent concurrent socket writes
991   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
992 
993   // Check connection status and get transport reference
994   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
995     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() check failed: active=%s, transport=%p",
996               atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) ? "true" : "false", (void *)g_client_transport);
997     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
998     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
999   }
1000 
1001   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1002   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1003   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() using transport=%p, is_connected=%s",
1004                   (void *)transport, acip_transport_is_connected(transport) ? "true" : "false");
1005 
1006   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1007   asciichat_error_t result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, type, data, len, 0);
1008 
1009   // Unlock after send completes
1010   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1011 
1012   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1013   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1014     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() send failed, calling server_connection_lost()");
1015     server_connection_lost();
1016     return result;
1017   }
1018 
1019   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1020 }

    ERROR_NETWORK, log_debug, log_debug_every,
    LOG_RATE_SLOW, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    packet_send_via_transport(), server_connection_lost(), and
    SET_ERRNO.

Sends terminal capabilities packet to the server including

height Terminal height in characters

1364                                                                                                             {
1365   // Log the dimensions being sent to server (helps debug dimension mismatch issues)
1366   log_debug("Sending terminal size to server: %ux%u (auto_width=%d, auto_height=%d)", width, height,
1367             GET_OPTION(auto_width), GET_OPTION(auto_height));
1368 
1369   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()
1370 
1371   // Build terminal capabilities packet locally
1372   // Detect terminal capabilities automatically
1373   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1374 
1375   // Set wants_padding based on snapshot mode and TTY status
1376   // Disable padding when:
1377   // - In snapshot mode (one frame and exit)
1378   // - When stdout is not a TTY (piped/redirected output)
1379   // Enable padding for interactive terminal sessions
1380   bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
1381   bool is_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1382   caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1383 
1384   log_debug("Client capabilities: wants_padding=%d (snapshot=%d, interactive=%d, stdin_tty=%d, stdout_tty=%d)",
1385             caps.wants_padding, is_snapshot_mode, is_interactive, terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty());
1386 
1387   // Apply user's color mode override
1388   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
1389 
1390   // Check if detection was reliable, use fallback only for auto-detection
1391   if (!caps.detection_reliable && GET_OPTION(color_mode) == COLOR_MODE_AUTO) {
1392     log_warn("Terminal capability detection not reliable, using fallback");
1393     SAFE_MEMSET(&caps, sizeof(caps), 0, sizeof(caps));
1394     caps.color_level = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
1395     caps.color_count = 2;
1396     caps.capabilities = 0;
1397     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.term_type, "unknown", sizeof(caps.term_type));
1398     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.colorterm, "", sizeof(caps.colorterm));
1399     caps.detection_reliable = 0;
1400     // Preserve wants_padding even in fallback mode
1401     caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1402   }
1403 
1404   // Convert to network packet format with proper byte order
1405   terminal_capabilities_packet_t net_packet;
1406   net_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.capabilities);
1407   net_packet.color_level = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_level);
1408   net_packet.color_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_count);
1409   net_packet.render_mode = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.render_mode);
1410   net_packet.width = HOST_TO_NET_U16(width);
1411   net_packet.height = HOST_TO_NET_U16(height);
1412   net_packet.palette_type = HOST_TO_NET_U32(GET_OPTION(palette_type));
1413   net_packet.utf8_support = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.utf8_support ? 1 : 0);
1414 
1415   const options_t *opts = options_get();
1416   if (GET_OPTION(palette_type) == PALETTE_CUSTOM && GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set)) {
1417     const char *palette_custom = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : "";
1418     SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.palette_custom, palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1419     net_packet.palette_custom[sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom) - 1] = '\0';
1420   } else {
1421     SAFE_MEMSET(net_packet.palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom), 0, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1422   }
1423 
1424   // Set desired FPS
1425   int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
1426   if (fps > 0) {
1427     net_packet.desired_fps = (uint8_t)(fps > 144 ? 144 : fps);
1428   } else {
1429     net_packet.desired_fps = caps.desired_fps;
1430   }
1431 
1432   if (net_packet.desired_fps == 0) {
1433     net_packet.desired_fps = DEFAULT_MAX_FPS;
1434   }
1435 
1436   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.term_type, caps.term_type, sizeof(net_packet.term_type));
1437   net_packet.term_type[sizeof(net_packet.term_type) - 1] = '\0';
1438 
1439   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.colorterm, caps.colorterm, sizeof(net_packet.colorterm));
1440   net_packet.colorterm[sizeof(net_packet.colorterm) - 1] = '\0';
1441 
1442   net_packet.detection_reliable = caps.detection_reliable;
1443   // Send UTF-8 support flag: true for AUTO (default) and TRUE settings, false for FALSE setting
1444   net_packet.utf8_support = (GET_OPTION(force_utf8) != UTF8_SETTING_FALSE) ? 1 : 0;
1445 
1446   // Set wants_padding flag (1=padding enabled, 0=no padding for snapshot/piped modes)
1447   net_packet.wants_padding = caps.wants_padding ? 1 : 0;
1448 
1449   // Set codec capabilities (client supports all codecs by default)
1450   net_packet.codec_capabilities_video = HOST_TO_NET_U32(VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1451   net_packet.codec_capabilities_audio = HOST_TO_NET_U32(AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1452 
1453   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1454   return threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES, &net_packet, sizeof(net_packet));
1455 }
